text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH 18F-FDG — WHOLE BODY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Chen is a 53-year-old woman with a history of HPV-positive (p16+, HPV-16 ISH confirmed) left base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma, T2N1M0 (AJCC 8th edition HPV-positive staging, Stage III), treated with definitive concurrent cis and intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT, 70 Gy in 35 fractions) completed approximately 12 months ago. She achieved a complete metabolic response on the 12-week post-treatment PET-CT. Study today is for 1-year post-treatment surveillance to evaluate for locoregional or distant recurrence.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body 18F-FDG PET-CT was performed from the skull vertex to the mid-thighs. The patient fasted for at least 6 hours prior to injection. Serum glucose at the time of injection was 88 mg/dL. Approximately 10.2 mCi of 18F-FDG was administered intravenously. Uptake period was 60 minutes. Emission images were acquired with low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic co-registration. Diagnostic-quality CT of the neck was performed with intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350, 80 mL) as part of the same session. Images were reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with PET-only, CT-only, and fused PET-CT reconstructions.

COMPARISON:
Prior PET-CT performed approximately 9 months ago (12 weeks post-chemoradiation completion). Prior CT neck with contrast from approximately 6 months ago.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND SKULL BASE:
No abnormal FDG-avid lesion is identified within the calvarium, skull base, or intracranial compartment. No new osseous lesions at the skull base. Normal physiologic FDG uptake in the brain.

ORBITS, SINUSES, NASOPHARYNX:
Symmetric, low-level physiologic FDG activity in the salivary glands bilaterally. The parotid glands appear slightly diminished in volume bilaterally compared to expected for age, consistent with established radiation-related parenchymal changes. Normal FDG activity in the parapharyngeal soft tissues. The nasopharynx is symmetric with normal mucosal FDG activity. Paranasal sinuses are clear. No focal hypermetabolic lesion.

OROPHARYNX AND PRIMARY TUMOR SITE:
The left base of tongue demonstrates no focal FDG-avid lesion. Mild, symmetric low-level mucosal FDG activity is present at the base of tongue bilaterally, consistent with residual post-radiation mucosal changes and well within the range of normal physiologic variation. No discrete nodular or masslike region of elevated uptake is identified at the primary tumor site. On the corresponding contrast-enhanced CT, the left base of tongue mucosa appears somewhat thickened and demonstrates mild heterogeneous enhancement without a defined soft tissue mass, all in keeping with chronic post-treatment fibrotic changes. No new soft tissue thickening to suggest recurrent tumor. The epiglottis, vallecula, and bilateral palatine tonsils are unremarkable. The hypopharynx and supraglottis appear symmetric without focal abnormality.

CERVICAL LYMPH NODES:
The previously documented left level IIA lymph node, which measured up to 2.1 cm prior to treatment and had decreased to under 1 cm on the 12-week post-treatment study, is now noted to be a small, non-enlarged node measuring approximately 0.6 cm in short axis, without FDG avidity above background soft tissue activity. No pathologically enlarged or FDG-avid lymph nodes are identified bilaterally in levels I through V, the retropharyngeal chains, or the supraclavicular fossae. Scattered tiny sub-centimeter nodes are noted in bilateral levels II and III, all without FDG activity, consistent with reactive or fibrotic residual nodes in the post-irradiation field.

SALIVARY GLANDS AND SOFT TISSUES OF THE NECK:
Bilateral submandibular glands appear mildly atrophic. No FDG-avid lesion. Scattered linear and reticular soft tissue densities in the bilateral anterior neck are consistent with cervical fibrosis in the treated field. No abscess or collection identified. The strap muscles and platysma are intact.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland appears mildly diminished in volume overall, without a dominant nodule. No focal FDG-avid lesion within the thyroid. This is noted in the context of the patient's known post-radiation hypothyroidism, currently managed with levothyroxine. Dedicated thyroid ultrasound is not part of this examination.

CHEST:
The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. No pulmonary nodules above the threshold for detection. No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy. No FDG-avid lesion within the thorax. Normal physiologic FDG activity in the myocardium and great vessels. The osseous structures of the thorax, including the ribs, sternum, clavicles, and thoracic spine, demonstrate no lytic or blastic lesions and no focal FDG uptake to suggest osseous metastatic disease.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
Liver: normal size and homogeneous attenuation. No focal hepatic lesion on CT. No FDG-avid hepatic abnormality. Normal physiologic hepatic background FDG activity.
Spleen: normal size. No focal FDG-avid lesion.
Adrenal glands: bilateral adrenal glands are within normal limits without nodule or FDG uptake above background.
Pancreas: unremarkable.
Kidneys and collecting system: symmetrically functioning kidneys with normal physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems bilaterally. No suspicious renal lesion.
Bowel: normal physiologic activity throughout the small and large bowel. No focal abnormality.
Lymphatics: no retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.
Bones: no suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion. No focal FDG uptake to suggest bony metastases in the imaged abdomen or pelvis. Normal age-appropriate marrow activity.
Uterus and ovaries: no FDG-avid pelvic lesion. Unremarkable uterus for age.

SKELETAL SURVEY (PARTIALLY EVALUATED):
The visualized skeleton, from the skull base through the mid-femora, demonstrates no focal bone destruction or FDG-avid lesion suspicious for metastatic disease. Normal background hematopoietic marrow activity throughout.

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid locoregional disease identified. The left base of tongue primary site shows no metabolically active lesion; residual mucosal thickening on CT is consistent with expected post-treatment fibrotic change without suspicious hypermetabolism.

2. The previously involved left level IIA cervical lymph node continues to decrease in size and demonstrates no pathologic FDG uptake. No cervical, supraclavicular, or retropharyngeal lymphadenopathy is identified bilaterally.

3. No distant metastatic disease. The lungs, liver, adrenal glands, bones, and remaining surveyed structures are without evidence of metastatic involvement.

4. Post-treatment changes in the bilateral neck, including soft tissue fibrosis, bilateral parotid gland volume reduction, and mild submandibular gland atrophy, are stable compared to prior studies and in keeping with the effects of prior definitive-dose radiotherapy.

5. Thyroid gland atrophy without focal FDG-avid lesion, consistent with known post-radiation hypothyroidism.

Overall, this 1-year post-treatment study demonstrates a sustained complete metabolic and anatomic response with no imaging evidence of recurrent or residual oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma.
